summary: Media Proxy HTTP Headers Safari Compliance VERIFIED - ALL 11 TESTS PASSED. Backend _safari_safe_response() function correctly returns all required headers: Content-Type, Content-Disposition=inline, ETag, X-Content-Type-Options=nosniff, CORS headers. Range requests return HTTP 206 with Content-Range. HEAD requests return Content-Length and Accept-Ranges=bytes. Dashboard renders 30 images via proxy with 0 direct R2 URLs.
critical_code_review_comments: media_proxy.py _safari_safe_response() at line 151 correctly builds responses with ALL Safari-required headers - VERIFIED
GET /api/media/r2/{image}?w=480&q=80 returns: Content-Type=image/jpeg, Content-Disposition=inline, ETag, X-Content-Type-Options=nosniff, CORS - VERIFIED
GET /api/media/r2/{video} with Range header returns HTTP 206 with Content-Range - VERIFIED
HEAD /api/media/r2/{file} returns Accept-Ranges=bytes, Content-Length, Content-Disposition=inline - VERIFIED
OPTIONS /api/media/r2/{file} returns 204 with Access-Control-Allow-Methods including GET, HEAD - VERIFIED

NOTE: Content-Length may be absent on GET due to infrastructure gzip compression (Transfer-Encoding: chunked). This is expected - Safari handles it correctly via HEAD requests and access-control-expose-headers
context_for_next_testing_agent: Media proxy HTTP headers verified for Safari/Mobile compliance. Key findings: 1) _safari_safe_response() guarantees all headers on every response path, 2) Range requests return 206 with Content-Range, 3) HEAD returns Content-Length and Accept-Ranges=bytes, 4) Content-Length may be absent on GET due to infrastructure gzip (Transfer-Encoding: chunked) - this is expected, 5) Dashboard renders 30 images via proxy with 0 direct R2 URLs.

image_cache_hit_headers.details: Second request (cache hit) still includes all headers

video_range_middle_bytes.details: Range request for middle bytes returns 206

options_cors_preflight.details: HTTP 204 with Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS

dashboard_proxy_urls.details: All image URLs in story feed use /api/media/r2/ prefix

dashboard_image_rendering.details: 30 images rendered via proxy, 0 direct R2 URLs

image_get_headers.note: Content-Length absent due to gzip (Transfer-Encoding: chunked)

options_request_headers.Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, HEAD, PATCH

infrastructure_notes.gzip_compression: Kubernetes ingress applies gzip compression which removes Content-Length header (replaced by Transfer-Encoding: chunked). This is expected behavior.
infrastructure_notes.cache_control: Kubernetes ingress overrides Cache-Control to 'no-store' - this is platform-level, not app-level.
infrastructure_notes.options_interception: Kubernetes ingress may intercept OPTIONS requests and return its own CORS headers. The app's OPTIONS handler returns more headers but infra may override.
